# [recovery-daemon] Rung-2 via B0 ŌĆö supervised-process restart (privilege-free)

**Parent:** Epic #13874. Graduated from Discussion #13871 (Axis B ŌåÆ B0).

## Problem / scope
A large share of "crash/hang" faults are **orchestrator-supervised** processes (the local model server, embeddings server, vector store) that `ProcessSupervisorService` already restarts past a cooldown ŌĆö zero new privilege. B0 makes the recovery daemon ACT on the `crash ŌĆö supervised process` class without the docker socket, shrinking the privileged surface to the external-container case only.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] **Rung-2 via B0:** restart an orchestrator-supervised process via `ProcessSupervisorService.superviseTask` + `RESTART_COOLDOWN_MS` (the `crash ŌĆö supervised process` cell).
- [ ] Escalates to Rung-3 (redeploy/page) if it restart-loops past N (anti-thrash, persisted).
- [ ] Covers the restart half of the `exhaustion` cell (Rung-0 shed + Rung-2 restart).
- [ ] Selects B0 (vs external) deterministically from `targetIdentity.kind === 'supervised-task'` (the C4 contract) ŌĆö no inference.
- [ ] Observability + verify-loop inherited from the daemon-core sub.
- [ ] **Zero new privilege** (AC-7 composition).

## Contract Ledger
- Adds: the B0 actuator binding + the `crash ŌĆö supervised process` / `exhaustion` policy cells.
- Reuses: `ProcessSupervisorService.superviseTask`, `RESTART_COOLDOWN_MS`.

## Sequencing
Blocked-by the daemon-core sub (daemon + dispatch). Privilege-free. **Fire-relief priority** ŌĆö covers a supervised local-model-server crash.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-06-23T08:34:23Z

## Superseded by ADR-0026 / #13900

Grace Cycle 2 on #13912 correctly caught that this ticket AC set drifted after ADR-0026 landed. The B0 supervised-process recovery slice is now explicitly narrowed to the supervisor in-process cooldown, and that live B0 path is already shipped by #13900 / #13882. Durable anti-thrash and the controller-agnostic `apply(serviceKey, action)` seam belong to the remaining B1 / privileged-actuator lane (#13884), not a second B0 service.

Closing this ticket as superseded rather than iterating the wrong-shape PR. Recovery-daemon sequencing should continue through #13884 against ADR-0026.

Related closure: #13912 closed as Drop+Supersede.

- 2026-06-23T09:19:41Z @neo-opus-grace cross-referenced by #13860
